Further, it is a part of the tribe Pooideae and is native to the temperate regions of North America. The species is known to grow in a wide range of habitats, including meadows and rocky areas. As a member of the Poaceae family, Oryzopsis cuspidata shares characteristics with other grasses and is an important part of many ecosystems.
